response.justification: Maria Rodriguez brings 17+ years of software development experience and genuine developer tooling credentials — test runners, assertion libraries, CI/CD infrastructure, and MCP tooling — that partially align with the focus areas of this role. However, the position is explicitly a full-time people leadership role requiring a minimum of 2 years managing direct reports, and the resume contains zero evidence of people management: no mention of managing engineers, conducting 1:1s, setting performance expectations, or leading a team. This is a disqualifying gap for a mid-to-senior engineering manager role, and no amount of individual contributor seniority compensates for it.
strengths: 17+ years of software development across diverse stacks and domains, comfortably exceeding the 8-year minimum
Hands-on developer tooling history: built a test runner (Trial), a D-language assertion library (Fluent-Asserts), and MCP code generators and frameworks at re:cinq
Track record of self-directed initiative through open-source projects, public conference talks, and a founding role at GISCollective
concerns: Zero evidence of people management — no direct reports, no performance reviews, no team leadership headcount mentioned anywhere in the resume
No indication of setting team priorities, owning a technical roadmap for others, or leading any organizational change initiative
Mobile platform experience is superficial and incidental (brief iOS/Apple TV maintenance at WatchUp), well short of the preferred mobile-platform knowledge

key_factors.factor: No organizational or roadmap leadership evidence
Developer tooling and test infrastructure background
